chunk_summary: Guidance on transitioning preterm infants to India's national iron and folic acid prophylaxis at six months, balancing therapeutic dosing and program schedules to prevent anemia and dosing errors.
learning_objectives: Understand the transition process from therapeutic iron dosing to national IFA prophylaxis for preterm infants at six months.
Identify risk factors requiring continued therapeutic iron dosing in preterm/VLBW/ELBW infants.
Learn how to reconcile iron exposure from medicinal drops, fortified formula, and complementary foods.
Communicate dosing changes effectively to caregivers and ASHA workers to prevent parallel dosing.
key_facts: India’s national IFA prophylaxis for ages 6–59 months includes 20 mg elemental iron and 100 µg folic acid twice weekly.
Preterm infants are at higher risk of anemia, requiring careful planning during the transition to avoid gaps or double dosing.
Continue mg/kg therapeutic dosing for high-risk infants with growth faltering, low dietary iron intake, or low hemoglobin/ferritin levels.
Transition to prophylaxis when growth stabilizes, complementary feeding reliably provides iron, and no clinical or lab deficiency is evident.
Age labeling must distinguish between chronological age (national schedules) and corrected age (growth/feeding decisions).
reference_quote: India’s national IFA prophylaxis for ages 6–59 months includes 20 mg elemental iron and 100 µg folic acid given twice weekly (National Health Mission, Government of India).
document_key_topics: Transitioning preterm infants to national IFA prophylaxis
Reconciling iron exposure from multiple sources
Preventing anemia and dosing errors in preterm infants
